businessSummary.oneLiner: World's largest EV maker and a 'Physical AI Platform' spanning autonomous driving, robotics, energy, and AI chips
businessSummary.description: Tesla has expanded beyond EV sales into FSD autonomous driving, Cybercab robotaxi, Optimus humanoid robots, and Megapack energy storage. In 2025, the company posted $94.8B in revenue with GAAP EPS of $1.08, achieving a 20.1% gross margin in Q4 2025, the highest in two years. FSD transitioned to a $99/month subscription model, and key catalysts including Cybercab production and Roadster reveal are set for April 2026.
howTheyMakeMoney: Energy storage and generation (Megapack, Powerwall, Solar)
Services and charging (Supercharger network, insurance, OTA upgrades)

keyMetrics.name: P/E (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Extreme premium vs S&P 500 avg ~23x and auto sector avg ~12x. Rose from 352x despite price drop, reflecting weak earnings

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: Based on 2026E EPS of $2.08. Over 4x the growth stock average of 30-40x. Prices in robotaxi and energy moonshots

keyMetrics.name: Revenue Growth (YoY)
keyMetrics.interpretation: 2025 revenue $94.8B, essentially flat. 2026 consensus projects 15% growth to $108.9B

keyMetrics.name: Market Cap
keyMetrics.interpretation: Down $30B from previous session. Still larger than Toyota, VW, GM, Ford combined, but shrinking

keyMetrics.name: Options RiskDex
keyMetrics.interpretation: CNBC reported options market signals downside risk outweighs upside expectations over next 30 days. Hit 1.92 last Friday, highest in 3 years

growthDrivers.title: Oil surges to $107, accelerating EV transition
growthDrivers.description: Iran rejected a 15-point peace deal and Trump warned of a 'final blow,' sending Brent crude to $107. Rising gas prices boost EV economics and accelerate consumer switching

growthDrivers.title: Optimus3 robot video unveiled, summer production start (3/25)
growthDrivers.description: Tesla revealed Optimus3 humanoid robot video showing reduction gears and precision hand design. Targeting 1M unit production rate by end of 2026. Trefis values the robot business at $2-3T

growthDrivers.title: Terafab $25B AI chip factory, Wedbush calls it 'game changer' (3/22-25)
growthDrivers.description: Wedbush maintained $600 target, calling Terafab a 'game changer.' AI5 chip limited production starts late 2026, full production in 2027

growthDrivers.title: Cybercab April production + Roadster reveal in late April
growthDrivers.description: Steering-wheel-free Cybercab robotaxi production and next-gen Roadster reveal both scheduled for April. Two major catalysts concentrated in a short period

growthDrivers.title: Netherlands FSD approval expected by 4/10, EU expansion
growthDrivers.description: Tesla expects FSD Supervised approval from Dutch authorities by April 10. If approved, EU-wide rollout possible by summer

growthDrivers.title: NHTSA FSD investigation escalation, regulatory risk (3/19)
growthDrivers.description: NHTSA escalated its investigation into FSD performance in low-visibility conditions. Trefis warned this could break the '$1 trillion AI thesis'

competitiveAdvantage.summary: 50%+ US EV market share, 8.4B miles of FSD data, Supercharger NACS standard dominance, Terafab for AI chip self-sufficiency

moats.description: 8.4 billion real-world FSD miles. Competitors cannot replicate this scale of autonomous driving training data in the short term
Supercharger network adopted as NACS standard. Ford, GM, and other competitors use Tesla charging infrastructure
Battery cells, AI chips (Terafab), software, and charging infrastructure all in-house. Cost advantage and rapid innovation
Synonymous with EVs. High brand recognition and passionate fan base generate demand without marketing spend

competitors.name: BYD
competitors.detail: China's #1, growing 100%+ in Europe. Price advantage rapidly closing the gap with Tesla

competitors.name: Zoox (Amazon)
competitors.detail: Expanding robotaxi in SF, Las Vegas. Austin and Miami trials started. Direct robotaxi competitor

competitors.name: Waymo (Alphabet)
competitors.detail: Leading in autonomous ride-hailing commercialization. 200K+ paid rides per week. Expanding service areas

competitors.name: Hyundai/Kia
competitors.detail: Expanding global EV share with Ioniq and EV6. Competing on value and quality

recentNews.headline: TSLA drops 3.59% to $372.11, breaks below 200-day moving average
recentNews.significance: Broader market selloff on Iran peace deal rejection. TSLA broke below its 200-day SMA ($395.89), triggering a technical bearish signal. Market cap shrinks to $1.40T

recentNews.headline: CNBC: 'Tesla stock true believers could be losing faith'
recentNews.significance: Options RiskDex hit 1.92, highest in 3 years, before settling at 1.75. Downside risk overwhelms upside expectations. EV policy support weakening and consumer enthusiasm fading

recentNews.headline: Q1 2026 delivery Wall Street consensus: 365,645 vehicles
recentNews.significance: 23-analyst average. +8% vs Q1 2025 but -13% vs Q4 2025. Full-year 2026 at 1.689M (+3.3%), a lackluster recovery

recentNews.headline: Trefis warns 'Tesla's $1 trillion AI thesis could break'
recentNews.significance: Questions NHTSA FSD investigation and Optimus 1M unit target. Current 180x+ FY26 P/E requires AI ambitions to materialize at scale

recentNews.headline: Optimus3 robot video released, pre-market up 2%
recentNews.significance: Optimus3 video featuring reduction gears and precision hand design. Summer 2026 production start planned

analystOpinions.notableComment: TipRanks 31-analyst average $399. Wedbush maintains $600 high, Needham at $25 low. Upside compressed from 10.7% to 7.2% as targets drift lower alongside the stock

risks.title: 200-Day SMA Breakdown, Technical Bearish Shift
risks.description: Broke below 200-day SMA ($395.89). Already under 50-day ($410.18). RSI at 37.5 approaching oversold but momentum is bearish. Options RiskDex 3-year high signals deteriorating investor sentiment

risks.title: Extreme Valuation Risk
risks.description: TTM P/E at 368x, 16x the S&P 500 average. Forward P/E of 179x is 4x+ growth stock averages. Any growth disappointment could trigger a steep selloff

risks.title: Q1 Delivery Growth Stalling
risks.description: Q1 2026 consensus 365,645 units, down 13% from Q4. Full-year 1.689M (+3.3%) falls far short of market expectations

risks.title: FSD Regulatory and Safety Risk
risks.description: NHTSA investigation escalated. California robotaxi application rejected. Trefis warns the '$1 trillion AI thesis could break'

risks.title: CNBC: 'True Believers Losing Faith'
risks.description: Options market shows downside risk overwhelming upside. EV policy support weakening, consumer enthusiasm fading, valuation burden creating a triple headwind

risks.title: Musk Risk (Brand Damage)
risks.description: DOGE political activity caused 45% drop in European January sales. Insider selling (Kimbal Musk $25.6M). Brand erosion directly impacting demand

buyReasons.title: Oil at $107 accelerating EV transition
buyReasons.rationale: Iran war escalation pushed oil to $107. Soaring gas costs make EVs far more economical, strengthening consumer switching motivation

buyReasons.title: April catalyst cluster (including 4/21 earnings)
buyReasons.rationale: Cybercab production, Roadster reveal, Netherlands FSD approval, and Q1 earnings (4/21) all concentrated in April

buyReasons.title: Energy business explosive growth
buyReasons.rationale: Megapack revenue doubled in 2025, now 12% of total revenue. High-margin recurring revenue reducing auto dependence

buyReasons.title: Approaching oversold territory
buyReasons.rationale: RSI 37.5 nearing oversold. Down 25% from 52-week high. Short-term bounce possible, though 200-day SMA breakdown is concerning

overallOpinion: Tesla broke below its 200-day moving average ($396), triggering a technical bearish signal. CNBC reports 'true believers are losing faith,' with options market downside risk hitting a 3-year high
Q1 delivery consensus of 365,645 is 13% below Q4, and full-year 1.689M (+3.3%) fails to show meaningful recovery. At 368x P/E, the valuation thesis is increasingly strained
April catalysts (Cybercab, Roadster, FSD Europe, 4/21 earnings) and $107 oil boosting EV demand provide bounce potential. Optimus robot and Terafab sustain the long-term growth narrative
At $372.11, RSI 37.5 nearing oversold but below both 50-day ($410) and 200-day ($396). If no recovery by 4/21 earnings, $340s possible. Better to watch than to chase

investmentVerdict.summary: The 200-day SMA breakdown and options market bearish shift are technically negative. But $107 oil boosting EV demand and concentrated April catalysts leave room for a bounce. Existing holders should hold; new entries are safer after 4/21 earnings confirmation

sources.name: CNBC - Tesla True Believers Losing Faith
sources.description: Options RiskDex 3-year high, downside risk dominant

sources.name: Trefis - Trillion Dollar AI Thesis at Risk
sources.description: NHTSA FSD investigation, 180x+ P/E justification questioned

sources.name: FinancialContent - Iran Peace Deal Rejection
sources.description: Iran rejects peace deal, oil $107, Nasdaq -1.14%

threads.hook: TSLA: 200-day SMA breakdown, options market turns bearish
threads.text:
$TSLA $372.11(-3.59%), market cap $1.40T

Tesla broke below its 200-day moving average ($396), triggering a technical bearish signal. CNBC reports 'true believers are losing faith' as options RiskDex hit a 3-year high.

Q1 delivery consensus 365,645 (Q4 -13%). P/E 368x. Growth recovery is too slow to justify the valuation.

But oil at $107 accelerates EV demand, and April is loaded: Cybercab, Roadster, FSD Europe, 4/21 earnings.

RSI 37.5 near oversold. Hold. Target $399 (+7%). 4/21 earnings is the inflection point.

witty.hook: When the true believers waver, is it the bottom or just the beginning?
witty.text:
$TSLA $372.11(-3.59%)

Broke below the 200-day SMA. CNBC says the faithful are losing faith. Options market most bearish in 3 years. The church of Tesla is seeing empty pews.

1/4
thread:
P/E 368x. To justify this, you need robotaxis, robots, AND a chip factory to all work.

Q1 deliveries 365K, down from 418K. A 'growth stock' with no growth. Awkward.

2/4

Oil at $107 does make EVs sexy though.
April: Cybercab, Roadster, FSD Europe, earnings.

The problem isn't the catalysts, it's the price tag

3/4

RSI 37.5. Almost oversold. But 200-day SMA is broken.
Is this the dip, or the beginning of the fall?

Hold. Let earnings on 4/21 answer

4/4

witty.hook: Tesla breaks 200-day SMA. Even the faithful are questioning the gospel.
witty.text:
$TSLA $372.11(-3.59%)

The 200-day SMA broke. CNBC says believers are wavering. Options market most bearish in 3 years.

P/E 368x with Q1 deliveries down 13% from Q4. When a 'growth stock' stops growing, the valuation becomes a liability.

Oil at $107 helps the EV case, and April is loaded with catalysts. But the church of Tesla needs miracles now, not just sermons.

RSI 37.5. Hold. Let 4/21 earnings be the test of faith.

empathy.hook: TSLA investors, the 200-day SMA break is scary, but hang in there
empathy.text:
$TSLA $372.11(-3.59%), down 25% from highs.

The 200-day SMA breaking is unsettling. CNBC coverage about 'losing faith' doesn't help either. The numbers look rough right now.

But oil at $107 genuinely boosts EV demand. April has Cybercab, Roadster, FSD Europe, and earnings on 4/21. These could change the narrative.

RSI 37.5, near oversold. When selling gets this extreme, bounces happen.

Target $399 (+7%). Don't panic. 4/21 earnings will clarify direction.
